disclaimer: ILLUSTRATIVE LOGIC ONLY. This decision tree is a demonstration scaffold and has NOT been validated for clinical use. It must be reviewed, edited, and signed off by a licensed clinician for each deploying organization before handling any real patient call.

EMERGENCY.label: Seek emergency care now
EMERGENCY.action: Advise the caller to hang up and call 911 (or local emergency services) immediately, then create a high-priority staff alert.

URGENT_CARE.label: Visit urgent care
URGENT_CARE.action: Direct the caller to the nearest urgent care, offer the address, and log the interaction for clinician follow-up.

CLINICIAN.label: Speak with a clinician
CLINICIAN.action: Warm-transfer to the nurse line or place a priority callback on the clinician queue, passing the full intake summary.

SCHEDULE.label: Schedule an appointment
SCHEDULE.action: Offer the next available appointment slots and book directly through the connected scheduling system.

rules.rationale: Acute safety risk. Route to emergency and crisis support; never delay.
Acute but not immediately life-threatening; same-day in-person assessment.
Needs clinical judgement but not emergent; route to nurse/clinician.
Routine, non-acute request suitable for scheduling.

default.rationale: When intent is unclear or no rule matches confidently, escalate to a human rather than guess.
